summary.brief:
16       New Zealand authorities currently require the Tribunal to assess the
culpability of a health practitioner  on the basis of
the civil standard of
proof, bearing in mind that serious allegations require a high level of proof. 
InBrake v Preliminary Proceedings Committee1a full Court of the High Court expressed  the standard  of proof in
the following way:. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: The Director of Proceedings alleged the patient presented to Dr S on 10 October 2003 with a bloated stomach and suffering abdominal discomfort.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ultimately the patient underwent surgery on 16 March 2004 at which time a large mucinous cystadenocarcinoma of the ovary was removed. The cyst weighed 14.7kg.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Tribunal was satisfied they constituted professional misconduct.
evidence_index.speaker: Tribunal conclusion

evidence_index.text: Doctor S has been found guilty of professional misconduct.
evidence_index.speaker: Tribunal conclusion

evidence_index.text: The Tribunal has assessed Dr S’s conduct in this case by addressing the tests noted in paragraph 25 in relation to the allegations in the notice of charge.
evidence_index.speaker: Tribunal analysis

evidence_index.text: Dr Marshall described the consultations of 7 November, 24 February and 11 March as recorded in Dr S’s notes, and suggested that “Dr S’s treatment of Ms L’s ovarian cyst was of the standard expected of a competent general practitioner”.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr Marshall

evidence_index.text: Dr Ngan Kee also thought however that by 24 February 2004 Ms L was likely to have “had a large cyst”.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr Ngan Kee

evidence_index.text: Among the matters noted in Ms L’s medical records are references to: In January, February and April 2003, I visited Dr S to have prescriptions filled and for other unrelated medical conditions.
evidence_index.speaker: Medical records

evidence_index.text: Ms L disputed the accuracy of this part of Dr S’s notes. She said she told Dr S she ate frequently because she found it impossible to eat a regular size meal because her stomach was compressed.
evidence_index.speaker: Ms L

evidence_index.text: The Tribunal has found Dr S breached the duty of care he owed Ms L in 12 respects identified in the notice of charge.
evidence_index.speaker: Tribunal finding

evidence_index.text: The Tribunal is in no doubt Dr S breached his duty of care to Ms L by failing to calculate her BMI and record that figure before prescribing Duromine.
evidence_index.speaker: Tribunal analysis

evidence_index.text: The charge was divided into five particulars and referred to Dr S’s care and treatment of Ms L during the period 10 October 2003 to 24 February 2004.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Tribunal’s primary goal is to rehabilitate Dr S. This objective would be put at risk if Dr S’s name were published.
evidence_index.speaker: Tribunal analysis

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reports bloating and abdominal discomfort since 11 November 2002. On 10 October 2003, she presented with a bloated stomach and abdominal discomfort. She had a history of palpitations and was on Sotalol and Ranitidine.

requestables.name: Blood Tests
requestables.description: Blood tests to exclude other pathology

reveal.clinical_significance: Could have helped identify underlying pathology
reveal.decision_impact: Failure to order was a breach of standard care

reveal.clinical_significance: Could have identified the cyst earlier
reveal.decision_impact: Failure to order was a breach of standard care

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with bloating and abdominal discomfort

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient presented with bloated stomach and abdominal discomfort
decision_points.prompt: What examinations and tests should be performed?

options.description: Perform abdominal and rectal examination, order blood tests and ultrasound
Prescribe Duromine without further examination or tests
options.clinical_reasoning: Inadequate assessment for prescribing weight loss medication

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant prescribed Duromine without adequate assessment
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant believed it was appropriate based on patient's weight concerns

expected_action_court.description: Perform comprehensive examinations and order necessary tests
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established these as standard care

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to perform adequate examinations and tests

patient_outcome.description: Patient underwent surgery for a large mucinous cystadenocarcinoma

key_findings: Failure to perform adequate examinations and investigations

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to perform abdominal examination and order tests
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper examination and tests could have led to earlier diagnosis

factual_timeline.event: Patient presents with bloated stomach and abdominal discomfort
Patient undergoes surgery to remove cyst
Patient presents with bloating and weight concerns

Failure to perform adequate examinations and investigations

court_findings.finding: Dr S breached duty of care
Dr S breached the duty of care
